55105.title: Erin Burnett OutFront
55105.description: It's Erin Burnett's turn to step out front and try to maintain a viable viewer presence for CNN in the weekday news arena. But the former CNBC anchor won't worry about ratings for her hourlong general news program, comparing that burden to "death." Instead, she'll concern herself with translating world events into relevant information for viewers, paying close attention to such topics as the Middle East and women's issues. "I want people to say that our show is smart. I want our show to be authentic", Burnett says in a promo. "I want our show to have courage". Left unsaid, of course, is that she wants her show to have a big audience, too.

55106.title: Anderson Cooper 360
55106.description: Cooper brings his prickly but often witty perspective to current events and personalities in the news. He is joined by a series of guests that frequently include political and legal analysts.

55107.title: CNN Special Report
55107.description: Bill Weir takes viewers to high tech farms, food labs, and chef's tables across America to find out how farmers, chefs, and fishermen have adapted in the modern world and interrogates the future of food production.

55112.description: Previewing the Olympic golf competition with a look at the history of golf in the Olympic Games. Plus, a look at Japan's unique golfing culture.

55113.title: Quest's World of Wonder
55113.description: Perched on the rocky cliffs above a shimmering sea are medieval ramparts, nearly perfectly intact, protecting a gem inside. Exploring the real heart and soul of Dubrovnik in Croatia.

55119.description: While spectators will be barred from attending the Tokyo Olympic Games, billions across the world will be using technology to tune in, making the Olympic Games a space for innovation.

55124.title: Smerconish
55124.description: After serving as an MSNBC contributor and guest-host on the network for four years, TV and radio personality Michael Smerconish believed he was ready to head a show of his own. CNN agreed, and thus was born `Smerconish', an hourlong Saturday morning program that takes after its host's independent views. Using a topical, conversational format, he aims for the TV show to be an extension of his weekday radio broadcast, with free-flowing topics and the ability to discuss issues that strike a chord with the audience. Along the same lines, he thinks it's important to present the content in an engaging manner, free of my-way-or-the-highway ideology. Smerconish, who has a law degree from the University of Pennsylvania, is also a New York Times' best-selling author.
